mnemonics: B.C.A.T. tracks Free Space: Bitmap, Contiguous, Allocation, Tables
keyPoints: Free space management tracks unused disk blocks using data structures like bitmaps or linked lists.
Common allocation methods include contiguous, linked, and indexed allocation, each with trade-offs in access speed and fragmentation.
The goal is to minimize fragmentation (external and internal) and efficiently allocate space for files.
commonMistakes: Confusing internal fragmentation (unused space within an allocated block) with external fragmentation (free space is broken into small, unusable pieces).
Assuming contiguous allocation is efficient for all file systems, ignoring its susceptibility to external fragmentation.
